Prior Art Search Report

Possible matches for professional review — not a legal determination
Paper · Process Searched 1574 patents + 267 papers 22 possible matches
Invention Summary
The manuscript discloses Dense Object Nets, a system comprising: (1) A fully-convolutional neural network (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) that maps full-resolution RGB images to dense descriptor spaces providing a D-dimensional descriptor vector per pixel; (2) A self-supervised training procedure using pixelwise contrastive loss with pixel matches/non-matches generated automatically from 3D RGBD reconstructions; (3) Object masking via 3D reconstruction-based change detection to focus learning on objects of interest; (4) Background domain randomization using autonomously acquired object masks to enforce cross-scene descriptor consistency; (5) Hard-negative scaling that adaptively normalizes the non-match loss by the number of active hard negatives rather than total non-matches; (6) Multi-object distinct descriptor learning via three strategies: cross-object loss, direct training on multi-object scenes, and synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering; (7) Selective class generalization or instance specificity achieved by modifying the loss function and sampling procedure; (8) Application to robotic manipulation: grasping specific points on deformed objects by finding best descriptor matches via L2 nearest-neighbor lookup, with class-general and instance-specific variants.
Search Overview
Novelty assessment found 197 prior art documents (after filtering self-references). Highest overlap: 20%. Risk level: CLEAR. 0 documents show LOW overlap, 197 are CLEAR. Closest prior art: "Fully self-supervised class awareness in dense object descri" at 20%.
Search Groups (6)
G1 Complete self-supervised dense descriptor pipeline for manipulation
Patent: ("learned descriptor" OR "learned visual feature") AND ("manipulation" OR "grasping" OR "pick and place") (206)
Scholar: dense object representation self-supervised robot manipulation (36)
G2 3D reconstruction-based self-supervision for pixel correspondence
Patent: ("contrastive loss" OR "contrastive learning") AND ("pixel" OR "dense") AND ("reconstruction" OR "3D") (290)
Scholar: self-supervised visual descriptor RGBD 3D reconstruction correspondence (52)
G3 Dense visual descriptors for robotic grasping specific points
Patent: ("descriptor matching" OR "feature matching" OR "correspondence") AND ("grasp" OR "manipulation") (293)
Scholar: dense visual representation robot pick and place object manipulation (50)
G4 Object masking + domain randomization + hard-negative contrastive
Patent: ("hard negative" OR "hard negatives") AND ("contrastive" OR "metric learning" OR "triplet") (290)
Scholar: hard negative mining contrastive loss visual descriptor learning (57)
G5 Multi-object distinct descriptors and class vs instance
Patent: ("cross-object" OR "inter-object") AND ("loss" OR "contrastive" OR "learning") (296)
Scholar: cross-object contrastive loss multi-object dense representation (42)
G6 Broader: self-supervised visual correspondence contrastive learning
Patent: ("correspondence learning" OR "descriptor learning") AND ("self-supervised" OR "unsupervised") (199)
Scholar: self-supervised descriptor learning visual feature correspondence (30)

Fully self-supervised class awareness in dense object descriptors
Paper · snYGnCdDpakJ
20%
method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD...fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB...The training procedure can be configured to produce either...the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose...The entire pipeline from data collection to trained...
Matched Points
✅
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Explicitly mentions RGB-D camera for dense object descriptor learning.
✅
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Describes DONs learning dense descriptor representation of RGB inputs.
✅
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Title mentions 'fully self-supervised class awareness in dense object descriptors', suggesting class-level descriptor capabilities.
✅
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Mentions using robot manipulator's pose to project 2D image pixels, suggesting depth + pose for 3D coordinates.
✅
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Title explicitly says 'fully self-supervised' for dense object descriptors.
Unmatched Points
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are...fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB...The entire pipeline from data collection to trained...
Matched Points
✅
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Mentions leveraging dense 3D generative model for correspondence - suggesting 3D-based pixel correspondence generation.
✅
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
References self-supervised visual descriptor learning for dense correspondence with CNN.
✅
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Explicitly mentions self-supervised visual descriptor learning.
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

Grasp2vec: Learning object representations from self-supervised grasping
Paper · E6J-cKl5LYcJ
4%
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained...
Matched Points
✅
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Describes self-supervised learning for robotic manipulation without human labeling.
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
About self-supervised grasping but not specific user-designated points across deformed configurations.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.
HeRO: Hierarchical 3D Semantic Representation for Pose-aware Object Manipulation
Paper · S_hETBRvm2oJ
4%
method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD...
Matched Points
✅
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Mentions RGB-D observations with object point clouds for manipulation.
Unmatched Points
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No TSDF/volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction mentioned.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No auto pixel correspondence generation from 3D reconstruction via raycasting.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No FCN for dense per-pixel descriptor mapping mentioned.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training mentioned.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Specific hinge formulation max(0,M-D)^2 not mentioned in title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No hard-negative adaptive scaling by active count mentioned.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No 3D change detection for object mask generation mentioned.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricted match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No background domain randomization mentioned.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation mentioned.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.

━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
Self-supervised learning of multi-object keypoints for robotic manipulation
Paper · FKlLBVJjwVgJ
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet.

A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.

A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.

The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.

━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About general hard negative mining in contrastive learning, not dense descriptors.

Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.

Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.

Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.

Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About keypoint detection, not dense pixel-level descriptors.
Semantic contrastive bootstrapping for single-positive multi-label recognition
Paper · Mughe9hmyoEJ
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multi-label recognition, not dense descriptors for manipulation.

Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About person re-identification, not object descriptors for manipulation.
Dgc-gnn: Leveraging geometry and color cues for visual descriptor-free 2d-3d matching
Paper · pi3uycLZNGsJ
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About descriptor-free 2D-3D matching, opposite approach to dense descriptors.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.

Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multimodal RL for manipulation with tactile sensing, not dense visual descriptors.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multimodal RL for manipulation with tactile sensing, not dense visual descriptors.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multimodal RL for manipulation with tactile sensing, not dense visual descriptors.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Not suggested by title/snippet. About multimodal RL for manipulation with tactile sensing, not dense visual descriptors.
Network training method and device, attitude prediction method and device
Patent · CN111783986A
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No mention of learning dense visual descriptors using RGBD sensor.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of TSDF or volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of a fully-convolutional network for per-pixel dense descriptors.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss with L2 distance and margin.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation max(0,M-D)^2.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of 3D change detection for automatic object masking.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization with object masks for descriptor invariance.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of training with multiple physically diverse scenes per object.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptors.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of descriptor distance threshold for match validation.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using matched pixel + depth + pose for 3D robotic grasping.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for cluttered grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of fully self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human annotations.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of FCN ResNet with bilinear upsampling for full-resolution descriptor maps.
An underwater target detection method based on self-supervised collaborative …

Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of 3D change detection for automatic object masking.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization with object masks for descriptor invariance.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of training with multiple physically diverse scenes per object.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptors.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of descriptor distance threshold for match validation.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using matched pixel + depth + pose for 3D robotic grasping.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for cluttered grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of fully self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human annotations.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of FCN ResNet with bilinear upsampling for full-resolution descriptor maps.
Training of text and image models
Patent · US20250173613A1
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No mention of learning dense visual descriptors using RGBD sensor.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of TSDF or volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of a fully-convolutional network for per-pixel dense descriptors.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss with L2 distance and margin.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation max(0,M-D)^2.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of 3D change detection for automatic object masking.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels via masks.

Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization with object masks for descriptor invariance.

No mention of training with multiple physically diverse scenes per object.

Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for cluttered grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of fully self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human annotations.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of FCN ResNet with bilinear upsampling for full-resolution descriptor maps.
LPCL: Localized prominence contrastive learning for self-supervised dense visual pre-training
Paper · WvtF12t24VsJ
—
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No mention of learning dense visual descriptors using RGBD sensor.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of TSDF or volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of a fully-convolutional network for per-pixel dense descriptors.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss with L2 distance and margin.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation max(0,M-D)^2.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of 3D change detection for automatic object masking.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization with object masks for descriptor invariance.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of training with multiple physically diverse scenes per object.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptors.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of descriptor distance threshold for match validation.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using matched pixel + depth + pose for 3D robotic grasping.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for cluttered grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of fully self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human annotations.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of FCN ResNet with bilinear upsampling for full-resolution descriptor maps.
